Name: Missing color task
Description: A color imagery task in which participants infer a missing color in a sequence of color stimuli by applying the same rule as in a reference sequence and then choose the correct option from two alternatives.
Instructions: Participants viewed two sequential color rows, inferred the missing color in the second row by analogy to the reference row, and selected the matching color option using the keyboard.
Comments: The task was designed to assess object imagery.
No feedback was given during the main trials.
Participants were encouraged to respond as quickly and accurately as possible.
